name: teammode
tagline: Orchestrates a named team of cooperating Codex agent workers, tracking durable on-disk state via a bundled Node script.
verdict: No security red flags in the SKILL.md text: it stays local (no credential reads, no network egress, no piped remote code), guards destructive team deletion with explicit stop/force rules, and is transparent about its own limits. The one caveat: it delegates real filesystem/git work (worktrees, branch merges, state deletion) to a bundled scripts/team.mjs that

highlights.summary: The skill delegates state management, git worktree creation, branch merges, and team deletion to `scripts/team.mjs`, which is bundled but was not included in this review (SKILL.md-only). Its actual co
Frontmatter has no version field or changelog signal; only `name` and `description` are present, so users can't tell if/when the skill changed.
